courses.title: Fundamentals of Neuroscience
courses.description: Electrical properties of individual neurons — foundational for understanding neural signal generation, which underpins EEG and any real-time biosignal processing system.

courses.title: Computational Neuroscience
courses.description: How neurons encode and process information — from single neuron dynamics to population-level computation. Mathematical modeling of biological systems.

courses.title: Digital Signal Processing
courses.description: Mathematical foundations of DSP — filter design, spectral analysis, and time-frequency methods. Directly applicable to audio, biosignal, and sensor data processing.

courses.title: Brain-Computer Interfaces
courses.description: End-to-end BCI systems: signal acquisition, feature extraction, classification, and real-time feedback. Combines DSP, ML, and systems engineering under strict latency constraints.

courses.title: Medical Neuroscience
courses.description: Clinical and anatomical neuroscience from a medical perspective — context for understanding what biosignal data actually represents in a real nervous system.

researchInterests.title: Embedded ML & On-Device Inference
researchInterests.description: Deploying trained models under real constraints: STM32, TFLite Micro, X-CUBE-AI, int8 quantization, UART output. Where ML meets hardware and software quality actually matters.

researchInterests.title: Real-Time Rendering & GPU Architecture
researchInterests.description: Physically-based rendering, Monte Carlo methods, denoising, and Vulkan pipeline architecture. Understanding the GPU as a massively parallel compute platform, not just a graphics card.

researchInterests.title: Neural Signal Processing
researchInterests.description: EEG preprocessing, artifact rejection, spike encoding, and spiking neural network classifiers. The engineering side of neurotechnology — real-time signal pipelines for neural data.

researchInterests.title: Production ML Systems
researchInterests.description: End-to-end ML beyond Jupyter notebooks: feature engineering, model training, deployment, monitoring, and failure modes. ML that runs in the real world under real conditions.

papers.title: Brain-Computer Interfaces: A Review
papers.notes: Canonical overview of BCI paradigms — useful for understanding the full engineering problem before diving into any one component.

papers.title: Optimizing Spatial Filters for Robust EEG Single-Trial Analysis
papers.notes: Foundational paper on Common Spatial Patterns — the standard feature extraction method for motor imagery classification.
